pubmed_links: [Bobeck et al. (2017) BigLEN-GPR171 system in basolateral amygdala regulates anxiety and fear conditioning](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28425495/); [Raddatz et al. (2025) GPR171 necessary for mood-related behaviors in males](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40318809/); [Fricker et al. (2025) ProSAAS neuropeptides and GPR171/GPR83 as therapeutic targets for anxiety](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/40450835/)
summary: GPR171 is a brain-expressed G protein-coupled receptor activated by the neuropeptide BigLEN; preclinical studies show it regulates anxiety-like behavior and contextual fear via the basolateral amygdala, and male knockout mice display altered mood-related phenotypes, positioning it as a candidate therapeutic target for anxiety and mood disorders. There is currently no direct genetic evidence linking GPR171 to autism, schizophrenia, bipolar disorder, or intellectual disability in humans. (emerging evidence)
